# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment that mounts host directories for our logging agent. The pod needs access to /var/log and /var/lib/docker to collect logs from all containers. This is for our centralized logging system that runs as a DaemonSet.

Prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: log-agent-deployment
spec:
  replicas: 3 # number of pods to maintain in the deployment (default is 2)
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: logging-daemonset   # label that select this Deployment for scaling up or down, default name 'logging' and namespace are set by kubelet if not specified in pod spec (default is the same as metadata) 
  template:    # Pod Template to create when new container starts. This will be replicated according with selector settings above  
      metadata:    
        labels:      
          app.kubernetes.io/name: logging-daemonset         # label for this pod, default name 'logging' and namespace are set by kubelet if not specified in Pod spec (default is the same as Metadata) 
    spec:  
      containers:    
        - image: your_image       # Docker Image to use. Replace with actual docker images here or pull from registry using name/tag, e.g., my-logging-agent:latest if you have a private repository in GCR (Google Container Registry) 
          name: log-agent         # Name of the container for easier debugging and scaling up later on  
      imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent    # Pulls Docker Image from registry when pod is started, default to 'Always' if not specified in Pod spec (default same as Metadata)
